gap_catalog.G0: No implementation or aggregate outcome evidence
gap_catalog.G2: Artifact fabrication and promotion are not delivered

meta.sp2_status: SP-DATA-ENVIRONMENTS (formerly SP-2) stays blocked until this documentation redefinition is independently reviewed and archived and its blocking decisions are resolved. Migration of the docs tree happens through ordinary SDD when undertaken; this plan is the portfolio, not a documentation-migration project plan.

decisions.blocking_effect: Authorizes semantic portfolio model; does not select open product choices
options: Reject and retain numeric SP model

decisions.blocking_effect: Blocks provider catalog and all first-provider changes
decisions.rationale: Minimal machinery for first delivery; matches current single-Docker reality. Multi-provider deferred until a second real provider arrives (YAGNI).
decisions.consequence: CAP-PROVIDER-CATALOG (per-instance registration/selection) deferred, not near-term; DPROV-* now select the single platform provider per kind rather than a first-of-many.

decisions.blocking_effect: Defines canonical tenant identity, project subordination, quota authority, and consumer boundaries
decisions.rationale: A customer/client is the sole tenancy unit; stable tenant_id separates technical scope from business labels and provider identifiers.
decisions.consequence: Project is the only normative subordinate scope in v1. PROD/QA/DEV are operational classifications, environment_family is not normative, and quota authority is defined once at CAP-TENANCY for consumers to use.

decisions.blocking_effect: Blocks data environments, recovery policy, and data-bearing workflows
options: default anonymize with audited QA/preprod exception
decisions.chosen: default anonymize with audited QA/preprod exception
decisions.rationale: Protect production-derived data by default while allowing explicitly justified QA or preproduction cases.
decisions.consequence: Non-production data is anonymized by default; every exception must be explicit, justified, approved, and auditable.

decisions.blocking_effect: Blocks deployment spec and remote target contract
options: target adapter owns routing DNS TLS by contract

decisions.blocking_effect: Blocks roadmap grouping and prerequisite ownership
decisions.rationale: Activate each prerequisite only when a concrete subproject requires it, avoiding a broad umbrella initiative without an immediate delivery need.
decisions.consequence: Cross-cutting capabilities remain independently owned and scheduled; no Platform Foundation program is created.

decisions.blocking_effect: Selects exactly one first database adapter
decisions.rationale: Reuses the existing tested local backend and minimizes initial delivery risk.
decisions.consequence: CHG-FIRST-DATABASE-ADAPTER targets Docker PostgreSQL first; managed-environment outcome contracts remain provider-neutral, and implementation stays blocked until required prerequisite evidence is accepted.

decisions.blocking_effect: Selects credential store and whether a secrets port is required
decisions.rationale: Approved as the smallest first-store path for opaque, ref-only credential handoffs; Vault and multi-store abstractions are deferred.
decisions.consequence: CAP-CREDENTIALS may create only opaque sops:// references for ref-capable targets. AC-CAP-CREDENTIALS-READY remains proposed until verification evidence is approved.

decompositions.rollback: deactivate if active; revert listed outputs
deactivate if active; revert listed outputs
